summary: Cocaine use explains multiple presenting symptoms including insomnia, agitation, diarrhea, dilated pupils, and sweating due to its direct pharmacological actions as a central nervous system stimulant. Given this comprehensive match between symptomatology and drug effect profile for cocaine toxicity, it stands out uniquely among potential poisons here. Agent2 reasoned through each symptom linking them directly back to well-known properties of cocaine such as causing sleeplessness, heightened alertness or anxiety (agitation), digestive issues possibly from vasculature constriction affecting gut function, eye dilation via stimulating adrenergic receptors throughout body systems responsible therefor; plus profuse perspiration reflecting elevated internal temperature resulting when metabolism speeds up under influence thereof – thus pinpointing cocaine overdose as most plausible cause based upon provided information alone without needing further diagnostic testing initially suggested by others but still recommended later if needed after initial assessment confirms suspicion raised herein today regarding substance abuse
